Test Case: Verify complete user registration, email verification, and login workflow
Pre-Conditions: System is running, email service is configured
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/auth/register with valid data
2. Retrieve OTP from database or email
3. POST /api/auth/verify-email with OTP
4. POST /api/auth/login with credentials
5. Verify JWT token from login matches expected structure
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, message contains "Registration successful"
Step 3: HTTP 200, token returned, verified=true
Step 4: HTTP 200, token returned, verified=true
JWT token is a valid Bearer token
Post-Condition: User fully registered, verified, and authenticated

Test Case: Verify complete workflow from browsing seminars to registering
Pre-Conditions: User registered and verified, seminars exist in database
Test Steps:
1. GET /api/seminars/upcoming (no auth)
2. POST /api/auth/login to get token
3. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with token
4. GET /api/registrations/history to confirm registration
5. GET /api/registrations/{id} to view details
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, list of upcoming seminars returned
Step 2: HTTP 200, JWT token returned
Step 3: HTTP 200, status=SUCCESS, seatsBooked=2
Step 4: HTTP 200, registration appears in history
Step 5: HTTP 200, registration details match
Post-Condition: Registration confirmed in database with SUCCESS status

Test Case: Verify user can register then cancel a seminar registration
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, future seminar with available seats exists
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with 1 seat
2. Verify registration status is SUCCESS
3. PUT /api/registrations/{id}/cancel
4. Verify registration status is CANCEL
5. GET /api/seminars/upcoming to verify seat count increased
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, status=SUCCESS
Step 3: HTTP 200, status=CANCEL
Step 5: availableSeats for the seminar increased by 1 compared to before cancellation
Post-Condition: Registration cancelled, seat returned to seminar pool

Test Case: Verify waitlisted user is automatically promoted when a SUCCESS registration is cancelled
Pre-Conditions: User A has SUCCESS registration, User B has WAIT registration for same seminar (seminar is full)
Test Steps:
1. Confirm User B has WAIT status registration
2. User A: PUT /api/registrations/{id}/cancel
3. GET /api/registrations/{waitId} as User B
4. Verify User B status changed to SUCCESS
Test Data:
User A token, User B token
User A registration ID, User B registration ID
Expected Results:
Step 2: HTTP 200, User A status=CANCEL
Step 3: HTTP 200, User B status=SUCCESS
User B receives promotion email notification
Post-Condition: User B promoted from WAIT to SUCCESS automatically

Test Case: Verify COMPANY type customer can register and view profile
Pre-Conditions: System is running
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/auth/register with customerType=COMPANY
2. Verify email with OTP
3. POST /api/auth/login
4. GET /api/users/profile
5. Verify customerType field in profile response
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, registration successful
Step 2: HTTP 200, verified=true
Step 3: HTTP 200, token returned
Step 4: HTTP 200, customerType=COMPANY in response
Post-Condition: Company customer profile accessible with correct type

Test Case: Verify user can register for multiple different seminars
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, at least 2 different future seminars exist
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId1}/register with 1 seat
2. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId2}/register with 1 seat
3. GET /api/registrations/history
4. Verify both registrations appear in history
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, status=SUCCESS for seminar 1
Step 2: HTTP 200, status=SUCCESS for seminar 2
Step 3: HTTP 200, history contains both registrations with correct seminarId values
Post-Condition: Two separate registrations created in database

Test Case: Verify system prevents registering for same seminar twice
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, already has active registration for seminar
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register (first registration)
2. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register again (duplicate attempt)
3. Verify error response
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, status=SUCCESS
Step 2: HTTP 400, message="You already have an active registration for this seminar"
errors array contains the same message
Post-Condition: Only one registration exists for the seminar

Test Case: Verify registration history only returns records within 1 year
Pre-Conditions: User has registrations older than 1 year and within 1 year
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/auth/login
2. GET /api/registrations/history
3. Verify all returned registrations have seminarDate within past 1 year
4. Verify registrations older than 1 year are not returned
Expected Results:
Step 2: HTTP 200
All seminarDate values in response are after (now - 1 year)
No registrations with seminarDate older than 1 year appear in results
Post-Condition: History filtered correctly by 1-year window

Test Case: Verify registration history is ordered by seminar date descending
Pre-Conditions: User has multiple registrations with different seminar dates
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/auth/login
2. GET /api/registrations/history
3. Verify seminarDate values are in descending order
Expected Results:
Step 2: HTTP 200
seminarDate of first item >= seminarDate of second item >= seminarDate of subsequent items
Post-Condition: Results returned in correct descending order

Test Case: Verify system prevents registration for seminars that have already occurred
Pre-Conditions: User logged in, past seminar exists in database
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/seminars/{pastSeminarId}/register
2. Verify error response
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Cannot register for a past seminar", "errors": ["Cannot register for a past seminar"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected for past seminar

Test Case: Verify system prevents cancellation after seminar has started
Pre-Conditions: User has registration for a seminar that has already started
Test Steps:
1. PUT /api/registrations/{id}/cancel for past seminar registration
2. Verify error response
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Cannot cancel a registration after the seminar has started", "errors": ["Cannot cancel a registration after the seminar has started"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Cancellation rejected for past seminar

Test Case Scenario: Unverified User Cannot Register for Seminar
Test Case: Verify unverified users cannot register for seminars
Pre-Conditions: User registered but email not verified, JWT token obtained via workaround
Test Steps:
1. Attempt POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with token of unverified user
2. Verify error response
Expected Results:
HTTP Status: 400
Response contains: {"status": 400, "message": "Email must be verified before registering for a seminar", "errors": ["Email must be verified before registering for a seminar"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Registration rejected for unverified user

Test Case Scenario: User Cannot Access Another User's Registration
Test Case: Verify user cannot view or cancel another user's registration
Pre-Conditions: Two users exist: User A and User B, User A has a registration
Test Data:
User B Authorization: Bearer <user_b_token>
Path: id=<user_a_registration_id>
Expected Results:
Step 2: HTTP 404
Response contains: {"status": 404, "message": "Registration not found", "errors": ["Registration not found"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Access denied, User B cannot see User A's registration

Test Case Scenario: User Cannot Cancel Another User's Registration
Test Case: Verify user cannot cancel another user's registration
Pre-Conditions: Two users exist: User A and User B, User A has an active registration
Test Steps:
1. User B: POST /api/auth/login to get token
2. User B: PUT /api/registrations/{userA_registration_id}/cancel
3. Verify 404 error
Test Data:
User B Authorization: Bearer <user_b_token>
Path: id=<user_a_registration_id>
Expected Results:
Step 2: HTTP 404
Response contains: {"status": 404, "message": "Registration not found", "errors": ["Registration not found"], "timestamp": "<timestamp>"}
Post-Condition: Cancellation rejected, User B cannot cancel User A's registration

Test Case Scenario: Re-registration with New OTP for Unverified Account
Test Case: Verify user can re-register with updated details if previous registration was not verified
Pre-Conditions: User registered but not verified
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/auth/register with same email but different name/password
2. Verify new OTP is generated
3. POST /api/auth/verify-email with new OTP
4. POST /api/auth/login with new password
Expected Results:
Step 1 (second): HTTP 200, registration successful
Step 3: HTTP 200, verified=true, name="New Name"
Step 4: HTTP 200, login successful with new password
Post-Condition: Account updated with new details and verified

Test Case: Verify available seats count is accurate after registrations
Pre-Conditions: Seminar with maxSeats=10 and 0 bookedSeats exists
Test Steps:
1. GET /api/seminars/upcoming to record initial availableSeats
2. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with 2 seats
3. GET /api/seminars/upcoming again
4. Verify availableSeats decreased by 2
Expected Results:
Step 1: availableSeats = N (initial value)
Step 3: availableSeats = N - 2
bookedSeats increased by 2
Post-Condition: Seat count accurately reflects the booking

Test Case: Verify available seats are restored after cancellation
Pre-Conditions: User has SUCCESS registration for a seminar
Test Steps:
1. GET /api/seminars/upcoming to record current availableSeats
2. PUT /api/registrations/{id}/cancel
3. GET /api/seminars/upcoming again
4. Verify availableSeats increased by seatsBooked amount
Expected Results:
Step 2: HTTP 200, status=CANCEL
Step 3: availableSeats = previous_availableSeats + seatsBooked
bookedSeats decreased accordingly
Post-Condition: Seat count accurately restored after cancellation

Test Case: Verify system correctly handles booking at exact capacity
Pre-Conditions: Seminar with 1 available seat remaining
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with seatsBooked=1 (fills seminar)
2. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register with seatsBooked=1 (second user, should waitlist)
Expected Results:
Step 1: HTTP 200, status=SUCCESS
Step 2: HTTP 200, status=WAIT (seminar now full)
Post-Condition: Seminar at capacity, subsequent registrations go to waitlist

Test Case Scenario: Cancelled Registration Excluded from Active Check
Test Case: Verify user can re-register for same seminar after cancellation
Pre-Conditions: User has CANCEL status registration for a seminar
Test Steps:
1. POST /api/seminars/{seminarId}/register (after previous cancellation)
2. Verify new registration is created successfully
Expected Results:
HTTP 200
New registration created with status=SUCCESS or WAIT
Previous CANCEL registration still exists in history
Post-Condition: New registration created, CANCEL status does not block re-registration
